prompt: Traditional Japanese ukiyo-e woodblock print in the style of Edo-period landscape artists. A lone traveler descends a winding mountain path, moving step by step down a steep slope toward a valley below. The terrain shows multiple ridges and valleys, with a nearer shallow valley and a deeper valley visible in the distance. Strong black contour lines define the landscape and figure. Flat areas of unmodulated color with no shading or gradients. Limited color palette of indigo blues, muted greens, soft ochre, and off-white paper tones. Stylized clouds and simplified trees. Asymmetrical composition with diagonal flow guiding the eye downward along the path. The traveler’s posture shows careful, incremental movement downhill, emphasizing step-by-step descent guided by the slope. Visible woodblock texture and slight ink imperfections. No modern elements, no text, no labels, no diagrams, no mathematical notation, no perspective realism, no photographic shading, no 3D rendering.
tradition_approach: Flat color, bold contour lines, asymmetrical composition, and limited palette replicate ukiyo-e grammar while a descending traveler encodes stepwise local optimization.
